Title: A highly conserved core bacterial microbiota with nitrogen-fixation capacity inhabits the xylem sap in maize plants

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Klebsiella variicola MNAZ1050; Citrobacter sp. MNAZ1397
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Biologically fixed nitrogen / ammonia-derived plant nitrogen
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots; stems; leaves, with strongest contribution measured in stems
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of the complete nif cluster in the two core diazotroph strains supports their capacity to fix N and contribute to maize N nutrition in planta
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: whole-genome sequencing of isolates; genome mapping showing entire nif cluster; acetylene reduction assay; 15N isotope dilution using SynCom inoculation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two core xylem diazotroph strains, Klebsiella variicola MNAZ1050 and Citrobacter sp. MNAZ1397, harbor the entire nif gene cluster. 2. These strains show high nitrogenase activity. 3. In SynComs they contribute to biological nitrogen fixation in maize, particularly in stems.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-term unfertilized control conditions favor higher abundance of diazotrophic core taxa
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome shotgun sequencing, PRJNA798255; 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing, PRJNA795590
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports use of core diazotrophic endophyte consortia as microbial biotechnology for maize N nutrition
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper does not resolve each nif gene individually beyond cluster presence and nifH-based detection

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Multiple enzymes of the tricarboxylic acid cycle
Common_Genes.Source_Species: Acinetobacter sp. ACZLY512; Rosenbergiella epipactidis YCCK550
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Biologically fixed nitrogen / ammonia-derived plant nitrogen
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirectly supports biological nitrogen fixation by helper strains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High abundance of TCA cycle gene reads in helper strains is associated with enhanced nitrogenase activity of diazotroph combinations, likely by promoting a microoxygen environment favorable for N fixation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genome read functional mapping; combination acetylene reduction assays with diazotrophs under reduced and ambient oxygen conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Helper strains ACZLY512 and YCCK550 contain more reads for major TCA cycle enzyme genes than other non-fixers. 2. Their co-culture with diazotrophs increases nitrogenase activity. 3. The effect is consistent with respiratory oxygen depletion aiding nitrogenase function.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient oxygen versus low-oxygen assay conditions influenced helper effects on nitrogenase activity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests inclusion of respiratory helper strains in SynCom design to support diazotroph performance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a single named focal gene; included because the study causally links helper metabolic gene enrichment to improved N-fixation performance

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Acinetobacter sp. ACZLY512; Rosenbergiella epipactidis YCCK550
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Biologically fixed nitrogen / ammonia-derived plant nitrogen
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirectly facilitates nitrogen fixation within SynComs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher abundance of ammonia assimilation gene reads in helper strains may facilitate diazotroph nitrogenase activity by consuming ammonia and favoring nitrogenase gene activation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: whole-genome functional mapping of helper strains; inference integrated with combination nitrogenase assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Helper strains ACZLY512 and YCCK550 contain more functional reads involved in ammonia assimilation. 2. The authors infer this may assist nitrogen fixation because ammonia consumption positively regulates nitrogenase gene activation/transcription. 3. These helpers enhanced nitrogenase activity when combined with core diazotrophs.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports rational inclusion of metabolic helper strains in N-fixing SynComs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Indirect mechanistic inference; no individual assimilation gene names are specified in the paper

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Indole-3-acetic acid (IAA) / root growth promotion trait
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Genome of K. variicola MNAZ1050 contains IAA biosynthesis genes, proposed as a mechanism contributing to increased maize root biomass after SynCom inoculation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: whole-genome sequencing; phenotyping of inoculated maize root dry weight and root/shoot ratio
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K. variicola MNAZ1050 genome contains genes involved in IAA biosynthesis. 2. SynCom inoculation increased maize root dry weight and root/shoot ratio. 3. Authors propose bacterial hormone production may contribute to root growth promotion.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SynCom inoculation in greenhouse pot experiment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential added value in SynComs by coupling N fixation with root growth promotion
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is proposed from genome content and phenotype association, not direct hormone quantification in this study

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Genome of Acinetobacter sp. ACZLY512 contains ethylene biosynthesis-related genes, proposed as a possible contributor to enhanced root development in SynCom-inoculated maize
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: whole-genome sequencing; plant growth phenotyping after SynCom inoculation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Acinetobacter sp. ACZLY512 genome contains genes involved in ethylene biosynthesis. 2. SynCom inoculation increased maize root biomass. 3. The paper suggests bacterial hormone production may participate in this synergistic growth promotion.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SynCom inoculation in greenhouse pot experiment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May help design multifunctional SynComs that support both N nutrition and root vigor
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic suggestion based on genome annotation; no direct ethylene quantification was reported

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Core xylem-associated diazotrophic bacteria (multiple taxa, including Klebsiella variicola MNAZ1050 and Citrobacter sp. MNAZ1397)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the ATP-dependent electron transfer component of the nitrogenase reaction required to reduce atmospheric N2 to ammonia
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Biologically fixed nitrogen / ammonia-derived plant nitrogen
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports maize nitrogen nutrition and growth
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Maize stem xylem; contributes to root, stem, and leaf nitrogen pools
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the plant-available nitrogen source via biological nitrogen fixation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher abundance/potential of nifH-carrying bacteria in maize xylem sap is associated with biological nitrogen fixation that contributes to maize nitrogen accumulation, especially in stems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: triple-qPCR of nifH/16S ratio; PCR detection of nifH in isolates; acetylene reduction assay; whole-genome identification of nif gene cluster in core diazotrophs; 15N isotope dilution in inoculated maize
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nifH-carrying taxa are enriched in maize xylem sap relative to other aboveground compartments under control soil conditions. 2. Core xylem isolates with nifH and nitrogenase activity were identified, including Klebsiella variicola MNAZ1050 and Citrobacter sp. MNAZ1397. 3. SynComs containing these diazotrophs contributed 11.81% of total accumulated N in maize stems.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functionally enriched in xylem sap microbiota, especially under unfertilized control conditions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-term fertilization regime; strongest enrichment/effect in unfertilized control soil
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing, BioProject PRJNA795590; whole-genome shotgun sequencing of isolated core strains, PRJNA798255
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a marker/function for selecting diazotrophic endophytes and SynComs to improve maize N-use and reduce fertilizer input
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper mainly tracks nifH as a marker of diazotrophic capacity rather than dissecting a single gene allele; full nif clusters were confirmed in two core diazotroph strains
